title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Relational Recall
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an audited service-metrics query pack after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Metric Validation
steps.instruction:
review case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 4 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. Competency probe: Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.

Inspect the Metric Validation trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Metric Validation.
